text: Biocatalysis is recognized as a key innovation for sustainable chemical synthesis and biotechnology. While enzymes have been widely used as biocatalysts at the ground state for the synthesis of many organic compounds over the last century, during the last quarter of a century photobiocatalysis, which utilizes excited state reactivity triggered by photoexcitation, has emerged as an increasingly diverse and powerful toolbox of new biocatalytic strategies. This review covers developments in photobiocatalysis for organic synthesis up to 2022, with the main aim of providing insight into the differences between the various approaches. Rather than providing a general overview of the state of the art in photobiocatalysis, we look ahead by systematically dividing photobiocatalytic approaches into activation mechanisms and their corresponding catalytic turnover mechanisms, highlighting associated mechanisms, substrate scope, and any enzyme engineering that has been reported. We aim to provide a guide that not only summarizes current photobiocatalytic strategies but also highlights potential future developments in the emerging field of photobiocatalysis.
